spec_match.rationale: The page now names the unchanged non-XSLT XML surfaces from ChromeStatus and exercises DOMParser.parseFromString(), XMLSerializer.serializeToString(), XMLHttpRequest responseXML, and fetch plus DOMParser handoff.

interactive.rationale: Chrome 148 DevTools MCP verified the MIME selector, parser button, all XML presets, and API-surface buttons update live DOM readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature index fuzzer framing while clarifying that this is a Rust engine swap with no new web-facing API.

edge_cases.rationale: The repaired fuzzer covers malformed XML parsererror handling, unsupported DOMParser MIME TypeError, internal entity expansion, external entity non-expansion, deep nesting, large attributes, and namespaces.

references.rationale: The references section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5309598397497344, WHATWG DOMParser, WHATWG XMLSerializer, and the feature conformance probe.

design_system.rationale: The added controls use shared design-system variables, no raw hex colours, and button background overrides pair foreground colours.

summary: Repaired after DevTools MCP reproduced that the old entity-bomb copy claimed DOMParser stripped DOCTYPE/ENTITY even though the browser expanded local entity text. Chrome 148 verified the final page: internal entity reports 10,000 expanded chars, external entity returns no text and makes no example.invalid request, malformed XML surfaces parsererror, unsupported application/json MIME throws TypeError, preset buttons recover to application/xml, API buttons log XMLSerializer, XMLHttpRequest responseXML, and fetch plus DOMParser results, the concept page has clean console/network state, and the feature conformance route reports 9 pass / 0 fail.
